description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a visually striking, textured appearance. The block is solid and robust, with a dark, almost black coloration interspersed with lighter gray marbling, suggesting a dense stone material such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the grooved top. The overall form is geometric and precise, indicating it was likely machine-cut for use in urban infrastructure. Its size and design suggest it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in public spaces, such as a sidewalk, plaza, or park.
description: A solid, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and a marbled black-gray coloration. The block is robust and visually striking, likely used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the parallel grooves across the top surface and the solid, smooth side.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the grooves running away from the viewer.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative obstacle
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
